heading: Independence at home medical practice demonstration program

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct a demonstration program (in this section referred to as the “demonstration program”) to test a payment incentive and service delivery model that utilizes physician and nurse practitioner directed home-based primary care teams designed to reduce expenditures and improve health outcomes in the provision of items and services under this subchapter to applicable beneficiaries (as defined in subsection (d)).

children.label: A
children.text: reducing preventable hospitalizations;

children.label: B
children.text: preventing hospital readmissions;

children.label: C
children.text: reducing emergency room visits;

children.label: D
children.text: improving health outcomes commensurate with the beneficiaries’ stage of chronic illness;

children.label: E
children.text: improving the efficiency of care, such as by reducing duplicative diagnostic and laboratory tests;

children.label: F
children.text: reducing the cost of health care services covered under this subchapter; and

children.label: G
children.text: achieving beneficiary and family caregiver satisfaction.

children.heading: Independence at home medical practice
children.label: b Independence at home medical practice

children.heading: Independence at home medical practice defined
children.label: 1 Independence at home medical practice defined
i
children.text: is comprised of an individual physician or nurse practitioner or group of physicians and nurse practitioners that provides care as part of a team that includes physicians, nurses, physician assistants, pharmacists, and other health and social services staff as appropriate who have experience providing home-based primary care to applicable beneficiaries, make in-home visits, and are available 24 hours per day, 7 days per week to carry out plans of care that are tailored to the individual beneficiary’s chronic conditions and designed to achieve the results in subsection (a);

children.label: ii
children.text: is organized at least in part for the purpose of providing physicians’ services;

children.label: iii
children.text: has documented experience in providing home-based primary care services to high-cost chronically ill beneficiaries, as determined appropriate by the Secretary;

children.label: iv
children.text: furnishes services to at least 200 applicable beneficiaries (as defined in subsection (d)) during each year of the demonstration program;

children.label: v
children.text: has entered into an agreement with the Secretary;

children.label: vi
children.text: uses electronic health information systems, remote monitoring, and mobile diagnostic technology; and

children.label: vii
children.text: meets such other criteria as the Secretary determines to be appropriate to participate in the demonstration program.

children.heading: Physician
children.label: B Physician
children.text: The term “physician” includes, except as the Secretary may otherwise provide, any individual who furnishes services for which payment may be made as physicians’ services and has the medical training or experience to fulfill the physician’s role described in subparagraph (A)(i).

children.heading: Participation of nurse practitioners and physician assistants
children.label: 2 Participation of nurse practitioners and physician assistants
A
children.text: all the requirements of this section are met;

children.label: B
children.text: the nurse practitioner or physician assistant, as the case may be, is acting consistent with State law; and

children.label: C
children.text: the nurse practitioner or physician assistant has the medical training or experience to fulfill the nurse practitioner or physician assistant role described in paragraph (1)(A)(i).

children.heading: Inclusion of providers and practitioners
children.label: 3 Inclusion of providers and practitioners
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed as preventing an independence at home medical practice from including a provider of services or a participating practitioner described in that is affiliated with the practice under an arrangement structured so that such provider of services or practitioner participates in the demonstration program and shares in any savings under the demonstration program.section 1395u(b)(18)(C) of this title

children.heading: Quality and performance standards
children.label: 4 Quality and performance standards
children.text: The Secretary shall develop quality performance standards for independence at home medical practices participating in the demonstration program.

children.heading: Establishment of target spending level
children.label: 1 Establishment of target spending level
children.text: The Secretary shall establish an estimated annual spending target, for the amount the Secretary estimates would have been spent in the absence of the demonstration, for items and services covered under parts A and B furnished to applicable beneficiaries for each qualifying independence at home medical practice under this section. Such spending targets shall be determined on a per capita basis. Such spending targets shall include a risk corridor that takes into account normal variation in expenditures for items and services covered under parts A and B furnished to such beneficiaries with the size of the corridor being related to the number of applicable beneficiaries furnished services by each independence at home medical practice. The spending targets may also be adjusted for other factors as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Incentive payments
children.label: 2 Incentive payments
children.text: Subject to performance on quality measures, a qualifying independence at home medical practice is eligible to receive an incentive payment under this section if actual expenditures for a year for the applicable beneficiaries it enrolls are less than the estimated spending target established under paragraph (1) for such year. An incentive payment for such year shall be equal to a portion (as determined by the Secretary) of the amount by which actual expenditures (including incentive payments under this paragraph) for applicable beneficiaries under parts A and B for such year are estimated to be less than 5 percent less than the estimated spending target for such year, as determined under paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: is entitled to benefits under part A and enrolled for benefits under part B;

children.label: B
children.text: is not enrolled in a Medicare Advantage plan under part C or a PACE program under ;section 1395eee of this title

children.label: C
children.text: has 2 or more chronic illnesses, such as congestive heart failure, diabetes, other dementias designated by the Secretary, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, ischemic heart disease, stroke, Alzheimer’s Disease and neurodegenerative diseases, and other diseases and conditions designated by the Secretary which result in high costs under this subchapter;

children.label: D
children.text: within the past 12 months has had a nonelective hospital admission;

children.label: E
children.text: within the past 12 months has received acute or subacute rehabilitation services;

children.label: F
children.text: has 2 or more functional dependencies requiring the assistance of another person (such as bathing, dressing, toileting, walking, or feeding); and

children.label: G
children.text: meets such other criteria as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Patient election to participate
children.label: 2 Patient election to participate
children.text: The Secretary shall determine an appropriate method of ensuring that applicable beneficiaries have agreed to enroll in an independence at home medical practice under the demonstration program. Enrollment in the demonstration program shall be voluntary.

children.heading: Beneficiary access to services
children.label: 3 Beneficiary access to services
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as encouraging physicians or nurse practitioners to limit applicable beneficiary access to services covered under this subchapter and applicable beneficiaries shall not be required to relinquish access to any benefit under this subchapter as a condition of receiving services from an independence at home medical practice.

children.heading: Starting date
children.label: 1 Starting date
children.text: The demonstration program shall begin no later than . Agreements with an independence at home medical practice under the demonstration program may cover not more than a 10-year period.January 1, 2012

children.heading: No physician duplication in demonstration participation
children.label: 2 No physician duplication in demonstration participation
children.text: The Secretary shall not pay an independence at home medical practice under this section that participates in .section 1395jjj of this title

children.heading: No beneficiary duplication in demonstration participation
children.label: 3 No beneficiary duplication in demonstration participation
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that no applicable beneficiary enrolled in an independence at home medical practice under this section is participating in the programs under .section 1395jjj of this title

children.label: A
children.text: located in high-cost areas of the country;

children.label: B
children.text: have experience in furnishing health care services to applicable beneficiaries in the home; and

children.label: C
children.text: use electronic medical records, health information technology, and individualized plans of care.

children.heading: Limitation on number of practices
children.label: 5 Limitation on number of practices
children.text: In selecting qualified independence at home medical practices to participate under the demonstration program, the Secretary shall limit the number of such practices so that the number of applicable beneficiaries that may participate in the demonstration program does not exceed 20,000. An applicable beneficiary that participates in the demonstration program by reason of the increase from 10,000 to 15,000 in the preceding sentence pursuant to the amendment made by section 50301(a)(1)(B)(i) of the Advancing Chronic Care, Extenders, and Social Services Act shall be considered in the spending target estimates under paragraph (1) of subsection (c) and the incentive payment calculations under paragraph (2) of such subsection for the sixth through tenth years of such program. An applicable beneficiary that participates in the demonstration program by reason of the increase from 15,000 to 20,000 in the first sentence of this paragraph pursuant to the amendment made by section 105 of division CC of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2021 shall be considered in the spending target estimates under paragraph (1) of subsection (c) and the incentive payment calculations under paragraph (2) of such subsection for the eighth through tenth years of such program.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: 6 Waiver
children.text: The Secretary may waive such provisions of this subchapter and subchapter XI as the Secretary determines necessary in order to implement the demonstration program.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: 7 Administration
children.text: Chapter 35 of title 44 shall not apply to this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall evaluate each independence at home medical practice under the demonstration program to assess whether the practice achieved the results described in subsection (a).

children.heading: Monitoring applicable beneficiaries
children.label: 2 Monitoring applicable beneficiaries
children.text: The Secretary may monitor data on expenditures and quality of services under this subchapter after an applicable beneficiary discontinues receiving services under this subchapter through a qualifying independence at home medical practice.

children.heading: Reports to Congress
children.label: g Reports to Congress
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct an independent evaluation of the demonstration program and submit to Congress a final report, including best practices under the demonstration program, including, to the extent practicable, with respect to the use of electronic health information systems, as described in subsection (b)(1)(A)(vi). Such report shall include an analysis of the demonstration program on coordination of care, expenditures under this subchapter, applicable beneficiary access to services, and the quality of health care services provided to applicable beneficiaries.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: h Funding
children.text: For purposes of administering and carrying out the demonstration program, other than for payments for items and services furnished under this subchapter and incentive payments under subsection (c), in addition to funds otherwise appropriated, there shall be transferred to the Secretary for the Center for Medicare & Medicaid Services Program Management Account from the Federal Hospital Insurance Trust Fund under and the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund under (in proportions determined appropriate by the Secretary) $5,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2010 through 2015 and $9,000,000 for fiscal year 2021. Amounts transferred under this subsection for a fiscal year shall be available until expended.section 1395i of this titlesection 1395t of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary estimates or determines that such practice did not achieve savings for the third of 3 consecutive years under the demonstration program; or

children.label: B
children.text: such practice fails to meet quality standards during any year of the demonstration program.

children.heading: Permissive termination
children.label: 2 Permissive termination
children.text: The Secretary may terminate an agreement with an independence at home medical practice for such other reasons determined appropriate by the Secretary.
